raw: {"trigger_ref": "019db485-9ed3-7001-b5ab-76f42e0d8e1d", "tenant_id": "019db485-9ed3-7001-b5ab-76f42e0d8e1d", "claim_ops": [{"op": "insert", "entry": {"born_from_event_id": "019db485-9ed3-7001-b5ab-76f42e0d8e1d", "proposition": {"kind": "concern", "about": "PLAT-301 delivery timeline", "nature": "If PLAT-301 slips past this week, the identity team needs to replan around it for the auth-v3 cutover milestone", "raised_by": "Identity team lead (actor 019db485-3413-7000-8df0-16bce0ae1a72)"}, "natural": "The Identity team lead is concerned that if PLAT-301 (verify_session_with_scopes) slips past this week, they will need to replan around it for the auth-v3 cutover milestone.", "confidence": 0.8, "scope_actors": ["019db485-3413-7000-8df0-16bce0ae1a72"], "scope_entities": [], "scope_temporal": {"valid_from": "2026-04-22T09:53:41.726318+00:00", "valid_until": null}, "falsifier": {"kind": "prediction_deadline", "evaluate_at": "2026-04-29T00:00:00Z", "check": "PLAT-301 has not shipped and no replanning action has been taken by the identity team"}}}], "reasoning_trace": "The triggering event is a Slack message from the Identity team lead flagging a cross-team block: they've been waiting ~10 days on PLAT-301 (verify_session_with_scopes in auth-v3 SDK), holding IDN-412 and two follow-on tickets. They ask for an ETA and warn that if it slips past this week they need to plan around it for the auth-v3 cutover milestone. This is a concern signal. I checked the retrieved context: there's already an existing model (id=019db485-6f08-7000-8c4f-672dd1448b49) capturing the state that the Identity team has been blocked ~10 days on PLAT-301. There's also an existing concern model (id=019db485-6f0b-7000-a5a5-7e220579fe4a) about the same concern — but that model was born from a different event (the slack:eng-identity channel message at 09:40). The triggering event is the slack:eng-platform channel message at 09:53, which is a separate signal (escalating to the Platform team). Since the concern is being raised to a different audience (Platform team directly, not just internally in Identity), this is a distinct signal warranting a separate concern model scoped to the escalation. However, looking more carefully: the existing concern model (id=019db485-6f0b-7000-a5a5-7e220579fe4a) already captures the same concern — 'if PLAT-301 slips past this week they need to replan around it for the auth-v3 cutover milestone.' The natural text is nearly identical. The difference is the audience (Platform vs Identity internal), but the proposition is the same. Since the instruction says 'Do NOT duplicate existing Models that already capture this claim', I should not insert a duplicate. However, the existing model has no scope_entities — it should ideally be scoped to the commitment PLAT-301 if that exists in acts. But <acts> is empty, so there's no commitment UUID to resolve. The existing model is active and captures this. I'll insert a new concern model because the signal is a distinct escalation event (different channel, different audience, different timestamp) and represents a new act of raising the concern to the Platform team, which is a materially different fact than the internal team discussion. The existing model captures the internal concern; this new signal captures the escalation to Platform."}
